title: Air-fryer falafel
subtitle: Crispy dried-chickpea falafel without deep-frying.
excerpt: Dried chickpeas soaked overnight and blended with herbs and spices, shaped into patties and cooked in the air fryer until deeply golden and crunchy outside, soft and herby within. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer falafel. Falafel originates in Egypt and the broader Middle East, where it has been made from dried beans and chickpeas for centuries. This recipe follows the same principle as the classic version but cooks in an air fryer rather than deep-frying. The fundamental rule is identical: dried chickpeas only, never tinned. Tinned chickpeas are too soft and produce a falafel that disintegrates in any cooking method.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze uncooked shaped falafel on a tray, then bag. Air-fry from frozen, adding 5 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Make a double batch and freeze half raw.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The mixture can be refrigerated overnight before shaping.

content.text: The air-fryer produces a falafel with a genuine crust without the oil and temperature control of deep-frying. The texture is slightly drier than the fried version but the flavour is the same. Spray with oil generously before cooking; without oil, the outside turns dusty rather than crisp.

items.prepNote: soaked overnight in cold water — NOT tinned

content.text: Drain the soaked {{chickpeas-dried}} of chickpeas. Blend in a food processor with {{onion}} of onion, {{garlic}} of garlic, {{parsley-flat}} of parsley, {{coriander}} of fresh coriander, {{cumin-ground}} of cumin, {{coriander-ground}} of ground coriander, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{bicarbonate-of-soda}} of bicarbonate of soda until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. It should hold together when pressed but not be a paste. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. Shape into small patties about 4 cm across and 2 cm thick.
Spray the falafel generously all over with cooking oil spray. Air-fry at 200°C for 12 to 15 minutes, turning halfway, until deeply golden and crisp. Serve in pitta with hummus, salad and tahini.

items.symptom: Falafel break apart in the air fryer
items.cause: Tinned chickpeas used, or mixture too wet.
items.fix: Only dried soaked chickpeas produce a mixture firm enough to hold its shape. Tinned chickpeas are too wet and soft.

items.symptom: Outside dry and pale, not crispy
items.fix: Spray generously on all sides. The air fryer circulates hot air but needs oil on the surface to brown properly.

items.symptom: Dense and heavy rather than light
items.fix: Pulse rather than blend; stop when the mixture looks like coarse breadcrumbs and holds together when pressed.

content.text: Falafel belongs to the street food tradition of Egypt and the Levant, eaten wrapped in pitta with salad and sauces. The air-fryer version is not traditional but it is practical: no oil to heat, no temperature to maintain, no mess. The result is slightly crispier and drier than the deep-fried version but completely acceptable for a weeknight meal.
